core_mechanism: asymptotic characterization of the finite-sample systematic bias of an estimator of a nonlinear (exponential-type) average, expanded in powers of 1/N via corrections to the central limit theorem, with heavy-tailed stable (Lévy) distributions governing the estimator's convergence
brief_reason: The abstract analyzes the inherent inaccuracy of estimating a non-linear average from N data values, giving its exact 1/N asymptotic behavior and a universal law derived from corrections to the central and other limit theorems in which stable (Lévy) probability distributions play the key role.
